era: Portland, 2007-present. Indie electronic/synth-pop band led by Joshua Hodges. Known for energetic live shows, danceable synth-pop, and a DIY ethos. Polyvinyl Records.

location.origin: Portland, Oregon. Founded as a solo project by Joshua Hodges in 2007.
location.formative: Portland's DIY infrastructure -- cheap rehearsal spaces, DIY venues, indie labels, audiences that reward authenticity over polish.
location.career: STRFKR -- indie electronic/synth-pop. Joshua Hodges. Portland. Polyvinyl Records. Albums: STRFKR (2008), Reptilians (2011), Miracle Mile (2013), Being No One Going Nowhere (2016). Legendary live shows -- costumed, confetti, joyful chaos. Indie Electronic at Genre Stage 78. E=5.

labels: Polyvinyl Records (primary label for major releases)

members.name: Joshua Hodges
members.note: Founder. Originally a solo project. The deliberately provocative name reflects the assumption it would never need to be a 'real' band name.

associated_musicians.name: ODESZA
associated_musicians.note: BACK-TO-BACK E=5! Consecutive electronic degrees. ODESZA is cinematic, festival-scale; STRFKR is scrappy, dance-floor-scale. Both E=5. Different architectures of electronic energy.

associated_musicians.name: Y La Bamba
associated_musicians.note: Both Portland-based. Y La Bamba is Latin-folk-experimental; STRFKR is indie electronic. Portland's genre diversity within the same indie infrastructure.

associated_musicians.name: The Postal Service
associated_musicians.note: Both electronic with indie sensibility. Postal Service is melancholic indie-electronic crossover; STRFKR is exuberant synth-pop. Different emotional registers.

associated_musicians.name: LAKE
associated_musicians.note: Both infectious, both joyful, both indie. LAKE is lo-fi pop; STRFKR is synth-pop. Same joyful spirit, different instruments.

associated_musicians.name: Mates of State
associated_musicians.note: Both E=5, both exuberant, both energetic live performers. Mates of State is organ-and-drums duo; STRFKR is synths-and-beats band. Same joyful intensity.

theory_nodes.concept: The synth-pop pulse (ninety-seventh acoustic element -- driving synth bass lines, pulsing electronic rhythms, catchy melodies layered over danceable beats; constant forward motion, no dramatic build, no cinematic arc, relentless joyful groove; the audience experience is physical: bodies moving to the pulse; distinct from euphoric build 95 cinematic tension-release and producer's palette 94 experimental studio exploration and shimmer veil 93 reverb-as-architecture)
examples: STRFKR (degree 96) -- driving synth-pop pulse. Constant forward motion. Relentless joyful groove.
Contrast with the euphoric build (degree 95, ODESZA) -- cinematic architecture with tension and release. The synth-pop pulse is flat, driving, joyful. Both electronic, different architectures.
Contrast with the producer's palette (degree 94, Erik Blood) -- experimental, genre-unbounded. The synth-pop pulse is pop-structured and dance-oriented.
Contrast with the shimmer veil (degree 93, NAVVI) -- reverb-as-architecture, intimate. The synth-pop pulse is upfront, dry, driving.
The engine adds the sound of relentless joyful groove.
theory_nodes.description: The synth-pop pulse is the 97th acoustic element. Driving synth bass, pulsing rhythms, catchy melodies. Constant forward motion. Relentless joyful groove. Dance-floor-scale.

ancestors: The euphoric build (degree 95, ODESZA) -- cinematic, tension-release. The synth-pop pulse is flat and driving.
The producer's palette (degree 94, Erik Blood) -- experimental studio. The synth-pop pulse is pop-structured.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-seven acoustic elements across ninety-six degrees. Track element 98 at degree 97.

theory_nodes.concept: The DIY maximalist (ninety-seventh artist-city pattern -- an artist who operates with DIY ethos: self-produced, independent, low-budget, while delivering maximalist energy in live performance; the tension between production values and live intensity; STRFKR records are scrappy; STRFKR live shows are explosions of energy, costumes, confetti, joyful chaos; Portland provides the economic and cultural conditions for the DIY maximalist)
examples: STRFKR (degree 96) -- DIY production, maximalist live shows. Costumes, confetti, joyful chaos.
Portland's DIY infrastructure: cheap studios, DIY venues, indie labels, audiences that reward authenticity.
Contrast with the Bellingham pipeline (degree 95, ODESZA) -- geographic origin and launch trajectory. The DIY maximalist is about production ethos and live energy.
theory_nodes.description: The DIY maximalist is the 97th artist-city pattern. DIY ethos meets maximalist live energy. Portland infrastructure enables the pattern.

ancestors: The Bellingham pipeline (degree 95, ODESZA) -- geographic origin. The DIY maximalist is about ethos.
The cross-genre bridge (degree 94, Erik Blood) -- connecting genres. The DIY maximalist is about tension between DIY and maximalist.
genealogy.relationship: Ninety-seven artist-city patterns across ninety-six degrees. Track pattern 98 at degree 97.

theory_nodes.concept: Indie Electronic at Genre Stage 78 -- synth-pop with indie sensibility, danceable electronic beats with DIY production values, catchy melodies with irreverent spirit; distinguished from Electronic (95) cinematic festival-scale and Electronic/Experimental (94) studio-as-instrument and Electronic Pop (86) structured melodic
examples: STRFKR (degree 96) -- Indie Electronic at Genre Stage 78.
Distinguished from Electronic (ODESZA, 95) -- STRFKR is scrappy, dance-floor-scale. ODESZA is cinematic, festival-scale.
Distinguished from Electronic/Experimental (Erik Blood, 94) -- STRFKR is pop-structured, melody-forward.
Distinguished from Electronic Pop (Postal Service, 86) -- STRFKR is exuberant synth-pop. Postal Service is melancholic.
Seventh electronic-adjacent genre in eleven degrees (86, 90, 91, 93, 94, 95, 96).
theory_nodes.description: Indie Electronic at Genre Stage 78. Synth-pop with indie sensibility. DIY production, danceable beats. Seven electronic entries in eleven degrees.

ancestors: Electronic (degree 95) at Genre Stage 77. Genre stages continue.
Electronic/Experimental (degree 94) at Genre Stage 76. The electronic era extends.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 78 at degree 96. Track at 97.

theory_nodes.concept: E=5 at degree 96 -- back-to-back E=5 with ODESZA (95); energetic live shows, national touring, Polyvinyl Records; legendary Portland live performances with costumes and confetti; highest energy tier
examples: STRFKR (degree 96) -- E=5. Legendary live shows. Polyvinyl Records. National touring.
BACK-TO-BACK E=5 with ODESZA (95). Consecutive maximum-energy electronic entries.
E=5 joins ODESZA (95), Sara Bareilles (81), Mates of State (80) at the energy ceiling.
theory_nodes.description: E=5 at degree 96. Back-to-back E=5 with ODESZA (95). Legendary live energy. Polyvinyl Records. Maximum energy.

ancestors: E=5 at degree 95 (ODESZA). Back-to-back maximum energy.

cross_references.relationship: BACK-TO-BACK E=5 ELECTRONIC! Consecutive degrees. Both electronic, both E=5. ODESZA is cinematic, festival-scale, euphoric builds. STRFKR is scrappy, dance-floor-scale, relentless groove. Different architectures of electronic energy.
PORTLAND CONNECTION. Both Portland-based. Y La Bamba is Latin-folk-experimental; STRFKR is indie electronic. Portland's genre diversity within the same indie infrastructure.
ELECTRONIC PARALLEL. Both electronic with indie sensibility. Postal Service is melancholic; STRFKR is exuberant. Both indie-electronic bridges; different emotional registers.
INDIE POP ENERGY PARALLEL. Both infectious, both joyful, both indie. LAKE is lo-fi pop; STRFKR is synth-pop. Same joyful spirit, different instruments.
E=5 EXUBERANCE PARALLEL. Both E=5, both exuberant, both energetic live performers. Mates of State is organ-and-drums; STRFKR is synths-and-beats. Same joyful intensity, different palettes.
